svg.text: plugin: claudster@claudster · marketplace: saajunaid/junai · commands: 7 · core: 39 skills · extras: 97 skills · agents: 12 · POSTER 2 OF 2
Use first when starting a new feature. Ask questions, surface unknowns, produce written spec.
Generates .claudster/plans/*.md. Phases map to /tdd cycles. Tracks progress across sessions.
Red → Green → Refactor per behavior unit
Core loop. Agents auto-fire. Repeat per plan phase. Do not skip red. ↺
Design-first: lock UI tokens before coding
Optional. Run before any UI /tdd cycle. Proposes font, colour, animation — prevents drift.
Auto-detects Gitea / GitHub Actions / local. CI-aware. Run after /tdd phase is green.
Manual. Run before every session close. Hook reads relay.md automatically at SessionStart.
Write or refresh relay.md at repo root
Creates/updates relay.md with full context. Use mid-session when state has diverged.
Wire hooks + CLAUDE.md for new repo
One-time per project. Generates the CLAUDE.md hierarchy, settings + status line, pre-push gate.
User-triggered, billed. /code-review ultra <PR#> for GitHub PR. Needs git repo.
claude plugin list / claude plugin details claudster
Captures baseline, verifies change against it, returns Evidence Bundle.
Use: hotfixes, DB migrations, security-sensitive changes. Evidence-first.
When: before applying anything where correctness > speed.
Flags staleness, duplication, misrouted how-to content. Shows diff first.
Use: every few weeks or when any CLAUDE.md exceeds ~80 lines.
Reviews diff for correctness, security vulnerabilities, style adherence.
Use: after phase is green and before commit/merge. Returns prioritised issues.
Reads broadly, returns audit-findings report + open questions.
Use: before architecture, major feature, or refactor in unfamiliar project.
Source-to-target mapping, transforms, ingestion, validation design.
Use: adding pipeline, integrating source, auditing data layer.
Reproduces and isolates bug, returns root cause + fix + regression test.
Use: when a test fails or behavior is wrong and cause is unknown.
Extracts non-obvious learnings, writes to instructions / runbooks / CLAUDE.md.
Use: after implementation, before context is lost. Writes docs only.
Checks file paths, symbol names, API shapes, dependencies. PASS/FAIL report.
Use: after plan is drafted, before any code is written. Read-only.
Severity-ranked findings. Auth/crypto/PII code, pre-merge security review.
Returns findings only — main thread applies fixes.
Reviews schema, queries, stored procs, indexing. Returns suggested SQL.
Use: slow queries, new schema, or before shipping hand-written SQL.
Write + run tests, compact pass/fail report
Writes tests, runs suite, returns counts + failures. Enforces TDD discipline.
Use: after implementing a phase or to add coverage. Can Edit/Write test files.
Screenshots at desktop + mobile. Returns structured UX/accessibility findings.
Use: after frontend phase, before shipping. Provide URL + optional design doc.
Write session state to relay.md — run before every close
Create or refresh relay.md mid-session or on demand
Discover requirements via dialogue, produce written PRD
Red-Green-Refactor cycle with strict TDD discipline
Lock UI design tokens before any frontend code (optional)
Wire CLAUDE.md + hooks + status line + pre-push gate (once per repo)
.claude/usage-log.jsonl — per-session token spend from the Stop hook
Persist a fact or decision across sessions via auto-memory system
CLAUDSTER-EXTRAS · 97 skills · disabled by default · enable on demand to keep core context lean
→ activate all 97 skills · restart Claude Code after
→ back to lean footprint · restart Claude Code after
Excel, Word, PowerPoint, PDF, VMIE slides.
CRUD + JWT auth + test suite scaffolding.
Schema design, Alembic migrations, query review.
Components, forms, tables, TanStack Query hooks.
CI pipelines, containers, web server config.
NSSM service wiring, IIS, PowerShell automation.
Auth patterns, API clients, access control.
Trace, profile, leak, and concurrency analysis.
Same SKILL.md format as Claude Code.
1. Always run /handoff before closing. relay.md is what saves you.
2. Run /setup-project-ai once per new repo to wire 4 hooks + the pre-push gate.
3. Use preflight agent after writing a plan and before writing code.
4. Extras off by default — enable only when you need the long-tail skills.
5. Agents are read-only advisors. They never edit code. Main thread applies their output.
plugin: claudster@claudster · extras: claudster-extras@claudster · See POSTER 1 for workflow lifecycle
Agents are read-only specialists. They spawn in isolated contexts, return structured
reports, and never write code. The main Claude thread applies their output.
Skills are prompt templates invoked by /skill-name. Extras add 97 more at the cost of
token overhead. Toggle extras per session — disable after to restore lean footprint.
Hooks require /setup-project-ai per project. Global hooks (branch warn, desktop notify) fire everywhere.
Skills NOT published to junai GitHub: validate_agents.py, vmie/* — run from agent-sandbox only.
